content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 9, 1990


Beth Pollard
Administrative Assistant
Town of San Anselmo
525 San Anselmo Avenue
San Anselmo, CA 94960

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-037

Dear Ms. Pollard:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding ap-
plication of the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

Does inclusion of the name of each councilmember in the
town's letterhead violate the mass mailing provisions of the Act?

CONCLUSION

Inclusion of the name of each councilmember in the town's
letterhead does not violate the mass mailing provisions of the
Act. However, the use of such letterhead must also comply with
the requirements of Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed).

FACTS

You submitted your request for advice in the standard let-
terhead of the Town of San Anselmo. On the left side of the page,
the stationery lists the names of five councilmembers, commencing
with the mayor. All the names are printed in the same typeface
and type size. The names are followed by each councilmember's
title. The name and address of the Town of San Anselmo are
centered at the top of the page. Four city offices with their
respective telephone numbers are listed on the right side of the
page. You have requested our advice to ensure that the town's
letterhead is in compliance with the provisions of the Act.

__________________________
1 Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-90-037
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Act defines a mass mailing as two hundred or more
substantially similar pieces of mail. (Section 82041.5.) Section
89001 provides that no newsletter or other mailing shall be
sent at public expense. Read together and taken literally, these
two sections could be construed to ban all mailings of 200 or more
substantially similar documents if the mailings are done at public
expense.

However, the above provisions do not constitute an absolute
ban on the use of names of public officials in agency documents.
When the stationery is used for mailings of less than 200
substantially similar pieces in each calendar month, the mass
mailing prohibitions of the Act do not apply. Conversely, mail-
ings of more than 200 substantially similar pieces in each
calendar month must comply with the requirements of Section 89001
as implemented through Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed).²

A mass mailing is prohibited when it either:

(2) (A) Features an elected officer af-
filiated with the agency which produces or sends
the mailing, or

(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or
other reference to an elected officer affiliated
with the agency which produces or sends the
mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the
elected officer;

(3) (A) Any of the costs of distribution is
paid for with public moneys; or

(B) Costs of design, production, and printing
exceeding $50.00 are paid with public moneys, and
the design, production, or printing is done with
the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.

Regulation 18901(a)(2) and (3).

_________________________

² The Commission approved the repeal and reenactment of
Regulation 18901 in December 1989. The regulation approved by the
Commission will be submitted to the Office of Administrative Law
in the near future. Although the new regulation is not in effect
until reviewed by the Office of Administrative Law and filed with
the Secretary of State, the new regulation represents the current
policy of the Commission.

File No. A-90-037
Page 3


     A mass mailing is not prohibited when the public official's
name appears only in the letterhead or logotype of the stationery,
forms, and envelopes of the agency sending the mailing.  (Regula-
tion 18901(b)(1).)  In any such mailing, the names of all elected
officers must appear in the same type size, typeface, type color,
and location on the page.  The mailing may not include any other
reference to the elected official such as the elected officer's
photograph, title, or signature except as specifically permitted
in Regulation 18901.

     The stationery of the Town of San Anselmo includes the names
of all elected officers in the same type size, typeface, type
color and location in its letterhead.  Accordingly, it is in
compliance with the mass mailing provisions of the Act.

     Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosure
